signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.55 holding above the critical 160.00 threshold where BoJ intervention risk historically intensifies. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% provides a +275bp OIS carry advantage sustaining the bid despite a bear steepener regime. USD/JPY vs VIX correlation at -0.124 (z=-2.03σ) confirms the JPY safe-haven function is broken; carry dominance persists while VIX remains at 17.9 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -105,238 reflects crowded carry positioning vulnerable only to acute risk-off or BoJ policy shift.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Macro Divergence + CESI + Positioning
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4057 supported by widening macro divergence as softer Canadian CPI (June) contrasts with sustained US data outperformance (USD CESI +48 vs CAD -3). Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% creates a +150bp carry advantage for USD. CAD COT LF net short -93,515 (long% 20.6%) is approaching historical extremes, suggesting the move is well-telegraphed but fundamentally backed by the +1.28% real rate spread favoring USD.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Technical Breakdown + Vol Premium + COT
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3417 testing downside targets after breaking the 200-hour moving average. Despite GBP COT LF net long +28,278, the options market is pricing significant protection with a 25d RR of -0.67 (put skew) and an elevated VRP of +4.5% (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.5%). Negative GBP CESI (-7) suggests data disappointment is eroding the SONIA-SOFR carry advantage (-11bp), increasing long-unwind risk.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — Retail vs Institutional Divergence
signals.text: AUD/CHF at 0.5675 exhibits extreme retail/institutional divergence with retail 92% short while RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0% maintains a +435bp carry premium. AUD COT LF net long +21,946 confirms institutional support for the carry trade. High VRP of +5.0% in AUD/USD suggests the market is paying for protection, but the carry-to-vol ratio remains attractive for AUD crosses while VIX is in normal territory.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Weakness
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1417 remains under pressure as EUR CESI (-16) reflects persistent data misses relative to the US. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25% provides a +150bp carry advantage to USD. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with a VRP of +2.4% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.7%) and a -0.44 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional preference for downside protection ahead of the July 23 ECB meeting.
